{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Systems at Scale {#sec-systems-at-scale}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- When one GPU is not enough: precision, parallelism, and memory tricks
     that turn many accelerators into one training run. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Mixed precision

<!-- fp16/bf16/fp8, loss scaling, what stays in fp32 and why. -->

## Data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism

<!-- The three axes; what each splits; communication costs; ZeRO/FSDP
     sharding of states. -->

## Memory-saving techniques

<!-- Activation checkpointing, gradient accumulation, offloading. The
     compute/memory trade-offs. -->

## Putting it together

<!-- A worked "how would you train a 1B model on N GPUs" sketch. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
